examples.title: Algorithm: Principal Component Analysis (PCA)
examples.description: Reduces dimensionality by finding the principal components of the data.
examples.code:
ALGORITHM PCA
INPUT: X (dataset)
OUTPUT: X_new (transformed dataset)

BEGIN
 1. Compute the covariance matrix of X
 2. Compute the eigenvectors and eigenvalues of the covariance matrix
 3. Select the top k eigenvectors corresponding to the largest eigenvalues
 4. Compute the projection matrix V using the selected eigenvectors
 5. Compute X_new = X * V
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the PCA algorithm, which reduces dimensionality by finding the principal components of the data.

examples.title: Python: Principal Component Analysis (PCA)
examples.description: Reduces dimensionality by finding the principal components of the data.
examples.code:
from sklearn.decomposition import PCA
import numpy as np

# Create a PCA object with 2 components
pca = PCA(n_components=2)

# Create a dataset with 3 features and 100 samples
X = np.random.rand(100, 3)

# Fit and transform the data
X_pca = pca.fit_transform(X)

print(X_pca.shape)
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates the PCA algorithm using scikit-learn, which reduces dimensionality by finding the principal components of the data.
